meta_title: For Students: email automation to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Build a Portfolio: Email Automation with AI (Advanced)
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about a blank portfolio. Learn to build an advanced email automation system using Antigravity and Claude Code to land your first dev role.
content.answer_block: To overcome the 'no portfolio' trap, build an automated outreach engine that manages personalized email campaigns. By leveraging Antigravity for infrastructure and Claude Code for iterative development, you create a real-world SaaS utility that demonstrates high-level proficiency in API orchestration, rate limiting, and intelligent content generation—the exact skills recruiters crave.
content.intro:
The silence of a blank portfolio is deafening. You have the skills, but no proof of impact. Employers aren't looking for another 'To-Do List' app; they are looking for engineers who can solve messy, real-world problems like scalability and asynchronous communication. 

Today, we pivot. We aren't just writing code; we are building an automated email engine that manages high-volume, personalized outreach. This project bridges the gap between 'student' and 'engineer,' giving you a narrative of technical competence that will command attention during your next interview.

steps.title: Architecting the Pipeline
steps.description: Define the system flow: CSV input, AI-driven content generation, and SMTP delivery. We focus on decoupling the logic to ensure modularity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a modular Python architecture for an automated email service that handles CSV ingestion, LLM-based personalization, and asynchronous SMTP delivery using asyncio.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --arch 'asyncio-based email automation pipeline with modular providers'

steps.title: Integrating LLM Personalization
steps.description: Connect the Claude API to dynamically rewrite email templates based on recipient data, moving beyond static placeholders.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer for Claude API integration that accepts custom prompts and recipient context to generate unique, human-like email bodies.

steps.title: Implementing Rate-Limited Delivery
steps.description: Prevent spam triggers and API bans by implementing a robust token-bucket rate limiter for outgoing SMTP requests.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a token-bucket algorithm in Python to rate-limit email dispatch to 50 emails per hour, ensuring compliance with SMTP provider limits.

steps.title: Data Validation and Sanitization
steps.description: Ensure your pipeline is production-ready by implementing strict Pydantic models for all incoming CSV data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Pydantic models to validate email, name, and company fields from CSV input, ensuring strict sanitization against injection attacks.

steps.title: Observability and Logging
steps.description: Add structured logging so you can debug failed deliveries in real-time, simulating professional SRE practices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure structured JSON logging for the email pipeline, capturing success, failure, and latency metrics for every outbound email.

pro_tips: Always use a 'test-mode' flag that routes emails to a Mailtrap account to avoid accidental spamming during development.
Implement a retry-backoff strategy using exponential backoff to handle transient SMTP connection errors gracefully.
Store your prompt templates in external YAML files to allow for rapid iteration without redeploying your core application code.
Use environment variables for all API keys, never hardcode credentials, and ensure your .env file is strictly in .gitignore.
Document your system's 'Why'—write a README that explains the architecture decisions; interviewers care more about your design trade-offs than the code itself.
content.next_steps: Deploy your app to a cloud provider like Fly.io or AWS Lambda, add a simple UI using Streamlit, and publish a detailed 'Building in Public' thread on X or LinkedIn to showcase your progress.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a builder with a deployed, production-grade system. This project is the differentiator that lands you the interview. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
